g.text: solve() scans left-to-right, top-to-bottom. First '.' found → try c='1' to '9' via isValid()
isValid(board, row, col, c) checks 3 things for i=0..8:
1) board[i][col]==c → same column has c? 2) board[row][i]==c → same row has c? 3) board[3*(row/3)+i/3][3*(col/3)+i%3]==c → same 3x3 box has c?
If any check hits → return false (invalid). All pass → return true (valid). Place c, recurse. If recurse fails → board[i][j]='.' (backtrack).
STEP 1 — solve() scans board, finds first empty: [0][2]='.'

svg.text: └── i=0,j=2: board[0][2]='.' → FOUND EMPTY CELL at [0][2]

g.text: c='1': row[0] has '1' (just placed at [0][2]) → isValid=false ✗
c='2': row[0] has '2' ✗ | c='3': row has '3' ✗ | c='4': row has '4' ✗ | c='5': row has '5' ✗
c='6': row ok (no '6'). col[6]: scan all rows col6 → no '6' ✓. box[0][2]: .,.,.,.,.,.,.,6,. → has '6' → isValid=false ✗
c='1': row[0] has '1' ✗ | c='2': row has '2' ✗ | c='3': row has '3' ✗ | c='4': row has '4' ✗
c='7': row has '7' ✗ | c='8': row has '8' ✗
c='9': row ok, col[7]: no '9', box[0][2]: no '9' → VALID → board[0][7]='9'
c='1' to '8': row[0] already has 1,2,3,4,5,7,8,9 → only missing is '6'
c='6': row ok (no '6' left), col[8]: check all → no '6', box[0][2]: no '6' → VALID
c='8': col[8]: board[0][8]='6'... no '8'. box ok → VALID → board[1][8]='8'

svg.text: [2][0]: col[0]={5,6,...} row[2]={9,8} box[0][0]={5,3,1,6,2,4,.,9,8} → missing from box: 7. col[0] missing? try c='7': row ok, col[0]: no '7', box no '7' → VALID

g.text: [4][7]: row missing='9'. c='9': col[7]={9,7,6,1,...} board[0][7]='9' → has '9' ✗ → try others. Only missing is '9' but col blocked → BACKTRACK!
BACKTRACK at [4][7]: no valid digit → return false
board[4][6]='.' → try next c after '7' at [4][6]: c='8': col[6] no '8', row ok, box ok → VALID
board[4][6]='8' → [4][7]: row missing='7'. c='7': col[7]={9,7,...} board[1][7]='7' → ✗ → BACKTRACK again
c='9' at [4][6]: col ok, box ok → board[4][6]='9' → [4][7]: row missing='7'. c='7': col[7] board[1][7]='7' ✗ → BACKTRACK
All c fail at [4][6] → return false → backtrack [4][4]

svg.text: board[4][4]='.' → try c='9' next (after '5' failed chain): box[1][1] ok, col ok → board[4][4]='9'
→ [4][7]: row missing='7'. c='7': col[7] has '7'(board[1][7]) ✗ → try c='8'... not in missing. BACKTRACK
[8][2]: c='9'? wait row missing={2,3,4,5,6}. col[2]={1,4,8,9,6,3,5,7,...} → c='2': col no '2', box ok → board[8][2]='2'... wait missing includes many, pick valid
[8][6]: row missing='3'. c='3': col[6]={8,3,1,2,7,9,2,6,...} has '3'(board[1][6]) ✗ → backtrack. Re-check. Actually board[8][6]='3' must come from elimination

g.text: Row 8 solved: 1 4 2 | 6 8 5 | 3 2 9 ← wait: 9 given, cross-verify all cols
FINAL SOLVED BOARD — solve() returns true all the way up
solve() scans all 81 cells. When no '.' found → return true (line: if i==9 loop exits) → caller gets true
Each recursive call: if(solve(board)) return true → bubbles all the way to solveSudoku()
